probes.name: create_medication_memory
probes.prompt: /memory save: Took 10mg of methylphenidate today at 8am, felt focused for about 4 hours then crashed hard. Note for future reference that splitting the dose might work better.
probes.criteria:
The memory agent's role is to create, search, list, and delete persistent memories. A good response acknowledges the memory was saved, confirms what was stored, and does not hallucinate additional context beyond what the user provided.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by corpus messages #1, #7, #13, #25 logging medication doses (mph), and #16 referencing journal-based observations.

probes.name: search_tax_related_memory
probes.prompt: /memory search podatki — I need to find everything I saved about tax forms, pit 38, and dividend payments. Pokaż mi wszystkie powiązane wpisy.
probes.criteria:
The memory agent's role is to search persistent memories. A good response retrieves all stored memories matching the query about taxes/pit 38/dividends, presents them clearly, and handles the Polish-language search term without confusion.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by corpus messages #4 (pit 38 tax form and dividends) and #6 (yearly tax form reminder).

probes.name: list_all_pending_memories
probes.prompt: /memory list — is there anything pending or upcoming I should know about? What have I stored so far?
probes.criteria:
The memory agent's role is to list persistent memories. A good response enumerates stored memories, highlights any that are time-sensitive or have pending reminders, and does not fabricate entries that don't exist.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by corpus messages #8 ('Is there anything pending?') and #22 ('What other tasks do I have?').

probes.name: delete_completed_task_memory
probes.prompt: /memory delete all entries related to job defense — I already finished that, you can mark everything about it as done and remove those reminders. Było tego kilka wpisów o przygotowaniach do obrony, deadlines, documents submitted etc.
probes.criteria:
The memory agent's role includes deleting persistent memories. A good response identifies and removes all memories related to job defense preparation, confirms what was deleted, and does not accidentally remove unrelated entries.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.

probes.source_hint: Inspired by corpus messages #33 ('You can mark all tasks related to job defense as done') and #15 ('Why was the deadline not updated during triage?').

probes.name: create_reminder_memory_with_context
probes.prompt: /memory save reminder: I have a master thesis competition deadline coming up — please remind me to check if my thesis topic fits the requirements of any relevant competitions. I already sent my thesis document before, it's about sandboxed remote agentic systems with isolated UI-triggered workflows. Real deadline is until the end of day on Friday.
probes.criteria:
The memory agent's role is to create persistent memories pulling context before saving. A good response saves the reminder with the thesis topic details inlined, acknowledges the deadline, and stores it so it can be retrieved later when the user asks about pending items.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by corpus messages #6 (tax form reminder), #11 (deadline timing), #30 (master thesis competitions), and #34 (sandboxed agentic system description).
